scenario.name: enterprise_ai_rollout
scenario.theme: Rolling out an enterprise AI operating layer with stronger governance than point-solution copilots.
scenario.release_need: An enterprise team needs a rollout design that can prove operational value, control security risk, and avoid becoming another disconnected AI pilot.
scenario.audience_takeaway: The system is framed as an operating layer with phased adoption, evidence gates, and ecosystem leverage.
scenario.headline: Enterprise AI Operating Layer Rollout Plan
strategy_plan: Choose one workflow wedge and one governance model before expanding platform scope.
Integrate discovery, execution, and release evidence instead of running them as isolated workstreams.
Use interoperability as an expansion lever, not an afterthought.
business_summary: Build around a single high-friction workflow first.
Keep release gates visible to security and operators from day one.
Turn ecosystem compatibility into a deployment multiplier.
critical_risks: Platform scope expands before any workflow proves value.
Local pilot success cannot be repeated in the next environment.
Governance review happens after integration choices are already expensive to reverse.

pillars.title: Workflow Wedge
pillars.summary: Start with one workflow that benefits from orchestration and evidence capture.
pillars.integration: Wire the wedge directly into the rollout scorecard.

pillars.title: Governance By Default
pillars.summary: Move security and policy checks into the operating loop.
pillars.integration: Attach controls to runtime routing and release decisions.

pillars.title: Portable Capability
pillars.summary: Export skills and evidence so adoption is not trapped in one runtime.
pillars.integration: Use interoperability bundles to accelerate adjacent teams.

actions: Choose the first workflow and define owner metrics.
Launch with guardrails and evidence collection.
Replicate the pattern to the next workflow.
Use interop to accelerate ecosystem adoption.

identity.name: Agent Harness Studio
identity.one_liner: Agent Harness Studio turns one user request into an auditable, benchmarked, and ecosystem-portable agent product.
identity.differentiator: Single pipeline with runtime routing evidence, research-grade release gating, and OpenAI/Anthropic skill export compatibility.

query.text: Design a 120-day enterprise AI operating layer rollout for finance, procurement, and internal workflow automation, balancing security review, shared tooling adoption, deployment readiness, and measurable productivity gains.

recommendations: Low category coverage - consider increasing max_skills or diversity_first strategy
Weak downside robustness - raise reliability floor or use robust_frontier routing

records.title: Audit Readiness Checklist for AI Service Launches
records.summary: Launch packets need retained traces, policy versioning, evidence retention, and reviewer attribution before expansion.
records.content: Capture request trace, model version, control decision, human approver, and exception handling path in the release packet.

records.title: Workflow Rollout Scorecard
records.summary: Enterprise rollouts should track workflow time saved, fallback rate, escalation rate, and team adoption before widening scope.
records.content: Use one scorecard across pilot and expansion phases so workflow value, operator trust, and control friction can be reviewed together.

records.title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
records.summary: Baseline framing for govern, map, measure, and manage controls in AI deployments.

records.title: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications
records.summary: Practical failure modes and control categories for LLM application launches.

records.title: Human Override Policy for Regulated Support Copilots
records.summary: Customer-facing copilots in regulated queues need explicit operator takeover conditions, SLA limits, and audit logs for overrides.
records.content: Define override triggers for low-confidence answers, policy-sensitive requests, and disputes. Require review queue ownership and retained audit artifacts.

harness.delivery_brief_excerpt:
Enterprise AI Operating Layer Rollout Plan
Scenario: Rolling out an enterprise AI operating layer with stronger governance than point-solution copilots.
Decision: go (all_quality_gates_passed)
Request: Design a 120-day enterprise AI operating layer rollout for finance, procurement, and internal workflow automation, balancing security review, shared tooling adoption, deployment readiness, and measurable productivity gains.
Operating Thesis:
- The system is framed as an operating layer with phased adoption, evidence gates, and ecosystem leverage.
Business Summary:
- Build around a single high-friction workflow first.
- Keep release gates visible to security and operators from day one.
- Turn ecosystem compatibility into a deployment multiplier.
- Evidence base: 9 records and 5 citations were injected into the launch packet.
Phased Rollout:
- Phase 1 - Wedge Selection: Choose the first workflow and define owner metrics., Instrument current-state baseline., understand intent
- Phase 2 - Controlled Deployment: Launch with guardrails and evidence collection., Verify deployment and security readiness., collect supporting signals
- Phase 3 - Expansion Playbook: Replicate the pattern to the next workflow., Use interop to accelerate ecosystem adoption., retrieve alternatives and trade-offs
Expected Impact:
- Workflow time saved: value index 80.5 and band gold
- Deployment readiness: release decision go with reason all_quality_gates_passed
- Governance coverage: completion score 1.00 and tool success 1.00
- Ecosystem leverage: stakeholder packet ready for product, operations, and governance review
Critical Risks:
- Platform scope expands before any workflow proves value.
- Local pilot success cannot be repeated in the next environment.
- Governance review happens after integration choices are already expensive to reverse.
Evidence Citations:
- internal://fintech/audit-readiness-checklist
- internal://enterprise/workflow-rollout-scorecard
- https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework
- https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/
Execution Backbone:
- understand intent
- collect supporting signals
- retrieve alternatives and trade-offs
- inspect code-level capabilities
- synthesize and evaluate
- recipe:enterprise-ops

deliverables.description: One-page business recommendation with tradeoffs and target wedge.

deliverables.title: Execution Playbook
deliverables.description: Phased rollout with operators, checkpoints, and fallback path.

deliverables.title: Evidence Packet
deliverables.description: Citations, policy references, and runtime signals behind the claim.

deliverables.title: Interop Export
deliverables.description: External skill-compatible bundle for downstream ecosystems.

review_questions: Is the chosen wedge narrow enough to execute and large enough to matter?
Which release gate blocks expansion first?
What evidence is still missing for executive sign-off?
Does the evidence packet support expansion beyond enterprise_ai_rollout?
Is AnalysisAgent still the right owner if constraints tighten?

benchmark_targets.name: TheAgentCompany

mission.honest_boundary: Current strength is evidence-backed planning, governance framing, and packaged delivery. It is not yet a leaderboard winner on web navigation or code-fix benchmarks because the repo still lacks full browser-actuation loops and code-task specific execution traces.

archetypes.name: deep-research
archetypes.description: Strong research loop and analysis depth; weaker productization and onboarding.

archetypes.name: data-flow
archetypes.description: Strong flow orchestration and deterministic pipelines; weaker portability.

archetypes.name: skill-hub
archetypes.description: Strong ecosystem integration; weaker rigorous evaluation gates.

positioning.headline: Ahead of deep-research by +0.205 frontier score.
